title: baccharis macrantha subsp. lloensis
intro: Baccharis macrantha subsp. lloensis, also known as the Lloa false heather or Baccharis Lloensis, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the Andean region of South America. The medicinal properties of Baccharis macrantha subsp. lloensis include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections and promote wound healing. Baccharis macrantha subsp. lloensis can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin.
